posts.title: Afghanistan's Midwifery Workforce: Crisis, Collapse, and the Road to Recovery
posts.summary: A data-driven analysis of maternal health, midwifery workforce, and the fragile pipeline sustaining care for millions of women in Afghanistan.
posts.body:
Afghanistan has one of the world's highest maternal mortality rates at 638 deaths per 100,000 live births. This report examines the state of the midwifery workforce — the primary safeguard for maternal survival — using national data to map gaps, inequities, and urgent policy priorities.

## Key Findings

- **77%** of deliveries in rural provinces occur without a skilled birth attendant
- **3×** urban-rural gap in access to trained midwives
- **40% projected decline** in midwifery pipeline under current conditions

## Why This Matters

Afghanistan's midwifery workforce is under severe strain due to political instability, funding gaps, and systemic barriers. Without urgent intervention, progress in maternal health gains over the past two decades risks being reversed entirely.

Read the full Dari-language report available for download on the Blog page.
